message:
test(fcp-core): pin policy-decision serde tag matrix (qyq9l)

Add crates/fcp-core/tests/policy_decision_serde_tag_matrix.rs.
PolicyDecision itself is a struct without serde derive; the two
decision-shaped enums that DO derive serde are CheckOutcome
(enforcement.rs:144 — Allow/Deny/Skip) and Decision (audit.rs:234
— Allow/Deny only). 16/16 tests pin both across JSON + CBOR.

CheckOutcome (tag = "outcome", snake_case):
- Allow JSON form pinned: {"outcome":"allow"}.
- Deny JSON form pinned: {"outcome":"deny", reason_code, explanation}.
- Skip JSON form pinned: {"outcome":"skip", reason}.
- All three variants round-trip through JSON.
- All three variants round-trip through CBOR.
- CBOR Map carries the `outcome` tag field with the snake_case
  variant label per variant.
- is_allow / is_deny / is_skip predicate truth table per variant.
- PascalCase variant names rejected; wrong tag key rejected;
  unknown variant rejected.

Decision (rename_all = "lowercase"):
- JSON form pinned: bare quoted string "allow" / "deny".
- JSON + CBOR round-trip preserve variant.
- CBOR form is a Text("allow")/Text("deny") value.
- PascalCase rejected; "skip" rejected (Decision has no Skip).

Cross-shape distinctness: Decision's bare-string JSON MUST NOT
deserialize as CheckOutcome, and vice versa — they share variant
names but distinct wire shapes.

JSON↔CBOR cross-format consistency: both formats decode to the
same PartialEq value for both enums.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
